Hey guys, listen, Jordan Bell, I cannot talk. My voice is completely shot. I'm so sorry, guys. I'm in Moscow. I thought I'd be able to do this speech. I'd be better in six or five days with this. You guys remember from the daily motivations, it was getting worse and worse. I have a doctor coming here. I think I know what it is. It's thrush. I got on my vocal cords every once in a while. So I'll be better in a couple of days. And listen, I have some as a special bonus. I got some great footage that I'm going to have my team dig up out of my archives. It's something no one has ever seen before. It's interviews that I did with a top psychologist that dissected my strategy. The topic I was talking about today was beliefs. So I'm going to dig into the archives for today. And you're going to love this stuff. It's really good. So it's so interviewing a professional psychologist that deals with the workplace. And finding out how my belief systems essentially allow me to be this top closer and how I've been able to help other people get rid of their limiting beliefs, break down beliefs using certain techniques. So guys, I'm sorry. I love trying to power through everything. But it would be a disservice to you at this point to try to take what you can and hear what I'm saying. So it's really hurting my, honestly, it's hurting my voice to even speak now. So I love you all. All right. I'll have this up there very short. I'm not going to talk about it directly. My apologies. But I promise you're going to love this. All right. Take care. Thanks. Jordan, much of the development literature talks about creating goals or a set of goals. To you, what's the difference between the vision and the goals? And how do you use the set of goals to achieve that vision? Goals to me are your stopping points along the way that tell you whether or not you're moving in the right direction towards your vision or you're essentially moving in the wrong direction towards your vision. So you have your vision, which is on top of everything. It's like this higher level function. It's this higher level target that you have, which really represents you. It encapsulates your beliefs, your desires, your wants, your hope for your future at this very high level. And there's not really a date on it. It's sort of just where you want to end up in your life. And as opposed to a goal, which has a start date and an end date, I set a goal today and it should be achieved by June 31, 2013. That's a goal. It has an end date. The vision doesn't. That's number one. Number two, I don't become emotionally invested in my goals. But I do become emotionally invested in my vision. To me, if I think about my vision and I read my vision to myself, if I don't get to that spot where I am almost, I feel my pulse beating through my wrists and the hairs in the back of my neck stand out, I don't get to that point of excitability and that point where I feel empowered and motivated. And my vision is not really the vision that I need to get what I want in life. I need to redo that vision to get me to that point to where I'm excited and pumped and motivated. When I look at my goals, I don't get motivated like that. I'm not looking to get motivated. I look at my goals, I'm looking for strategy of maybe managing time and how much effort. What are the essentially what people don't need to meet to help me achieve this goal? What processes do I have to learn if there are any processes? What bodies of knowledge do I need to familiarize myself with to achieve a goal? So a goal is sort of a very, it's almost a logical point that you're moving towards while the vision's more of an emotional point you're moving towards. And I think that the two together are incredibly powerful. So I'm not, you know, back in the day of, you know, we had this amazing, actually it was a recording back then in the 60s, it was by a man named Earl Nightingale, and he, Earl Nightingale. And it was called The Stranger's Secret. And basically at the heart of the book was this idea, which back then was revolutionary, is that you become what you think about, that you can visualize where you want to go and you can essentially manifest that in your life. Back then that was groundbreaking. And at the heart of Earl Nightingale's formula was this sort of goal-setting mentality. To set goals, right, and then to move towards those goals and have them as your targets. And he used this study which turned out to not be true, by the way, and they had taken some study at Yale University and the 5% of the people at Yale in the graduating class of 1940 who set goals ended up making more money than the other 95% of Yale who didn't set goals. It turned out not to be true, but that was a famous study for how it turned out to be false. The point was this though, and it is true, is that people who do set goals will tend to be much more successful and people who don't set goals strictly because a goal has a target. And it's very hard to almost hit something that you can't see. You know, a target in your life, good luck trying to get somewhere. You see someone like a random preacher. What happened was is that in the 1960s, that was earth shattering stuff. People would say, I'm goal-oriented. I'm going to set goals and it really worked. Over the years, it became bastardized. This idea of goal-setting became so commonplace that everyone became goal-oriented. And if you look at anyone's resume, I'm a goal-oriented individual, blah, blah, blah. And it means nothing. You know, how many times have we all set goals and not achieved them? Everyone's raised their hand. We almost become conditioned not to achieve our goals. So the power that the strangest secret used to have, which was immense and based on goals, has now lost its power because we have essentially bastardized the word goals. Now it's about vision. It's transcended to that next level up, which means you have to have your vision. That's what empowers you. The goals now are the stopping point, essentially, the map along the way, the sign that says, I'm heading towards the destination. Whether I achieve it or not, it's not going to dictate whether I'm happy or sad in life. It just tells me if I'm heading in the right direction. If I'm heading towards my vision, I'll feel some pleasure out of it. If I feel I'm heading away from my vision, I'll feel distressed and that causes me to want to work harder and to figure out a new strategy. But I'm always constantly setting goals, abandoning goals that haven't worked, resetting one. And then if a goal works, I move to the next goal, but the vision is that sort of that shining light that stands above it all. John, can you go through the specific steps that someone would need to go through to set their goals? Sure, I mean, very simple. To me, goal setting is a mathematical equation and it starts like this. Set a goal, number one. Number two, put a date on it. Every goal has to have a date. Then you have short-term goals, which could be in, you know, every day you could have goals, end of day goals, right? You could make them or not make them by the end of the day and then you discard them and set new goals the next day. So those are ultra short-term goals. But you have short-term goals that are like, one month goals, you have three month goals, you have intermediate goals that are like maybe a year and you have long-term goals that are three years. And then, you know, past two, three years, you start getting into sort of more, to me, the vision aspect of things, right? But those are the various goals. They have dates on them. Step three, so number one, set a goal. Two, put a date on it. Step three, identify the people, groups and organizations that you need to align yourself with to achieve your goal. In other words, in any goal out there, typically you're not gonna do it in a vacuum. There's people you need to know, organizations you might need to join, employees you need to know how, or whatever it might be, identify all those peoples, those groups, those organizations that you need to have access to. Step four, learn what you need to know. Figure out, is there stuff I gotta learn? And if there is, educate yourself. Find out what you or hire someone that essentially knows what you need to know. So again, let's go through it. Step one, set a goal. Step two, put a date on it. Step three, identify the peoples, groups and organizations that you need to align yourself with. Step four, learn what you need to know. Step five, develop a plan, meaning the best plan available based on the information you currently have. There are no perfect plans. You don't wanna be one of these people that develops a plan forever. I'm still on the planning stage. When I'm done planning, then I'll get started. And there is no perfect plan because the plan that you end up developing will probably change four or five times before you actually end up getting what you want. So but you have to have a plan. So you don't have the best plan available based on the information you currently have about the market, about business itself, and your experience. Step six, take massive action. Not a little bit of action. Take massive action towards that goal and go out and do what you gotta do. Step seven, do what's called have visual acuity. Analyze your results, see what's working, see what's not working. Anytime we take actions towards a goal, we'll find that some of those actions are actually getting us what we want. And in fact, probably more of those actions in the beginning aren't getting us what we want. So you wanna actually analyze your results, see what's working, what's not, and change your approach, which is step eight, is one step at a time, change your approach. So we don't wanna change everything at once, but if it's not working, you change one thing, see if it's working again, and you keep running that loop, change your approach, see what's working, change your approach. How many times do you change your approach until you get what you want until you see yourself moving in the right direction? Now, there is one caveat to that, is that obviously you don't wanna be a moron who's bagging his head against the wolf, or his head against the wolf forever, so for a certain amount of times of change your approach, if you're still not getting what you want in life, you have to say, hmm, maybe there's something wrong with the actual goal. Maybe the goal was flawed, maybe I'm missing some of the strategies myself, or it doesn't occur in a vacuum. After a certain point in time, you don't wanna get, and by the way, this is the danger of becoming emotionally invested in your goals, because if you become so emotionally invested in your goal, and you're not achieving it, you'll become irrational, you'll irrationally be chasing after your goal, you'll keep changing your approach, and rather than saying, maybe you should abandon the goals, maybe it's a bad business, maybe it's a bad idea altogether, so that's why you wanna keep rational instead of becoming irrational and getting emotionally attached to the goal. So those are the steps, let's go through one more time, step one, set a goal, step two, put a date on it, step three, identify the people's groups and organizations you need to alarm yourself with, step four, learn what you gotta learn, know what you gotta know, step five, develop a plan, attack, step six, take massive action, right? Step seven, analyze your results, and lastly, see what's working and what's not, change your approach. But simple as that. Although it's been proven that 90% of people don't follow through on their goals. So what's the difference here? How do you have such a higher level of success with people following through and achieving their goals using the straight line system? Number one, I'm not sure you got that number of 90% for, but it probably sounds pretty accurate. So let's just assume that that is accurate. I would say the problem is, for the most part, is that most people who do set goals don't do it that way. They don't actually set goals, put dates on them, figure out who they gotta know to achieve the goal, what they gotta learn to achieve the goal, they don't develop a great plan, or they've spent too much time on the plan, and they try to develop the perfect plan when there is none, and they don't have that sensory acuity to keep looking at it and changing their approach and essentially cultivating the art of flexibility, learning how to be flexible when you take action. So you see what's working and what's not, and don't say, you know, no, I'm gonna keep doing this and banging my head against the wall whether I like it or not, because that's my plan. My plan's a good plan. So that's, I think, a lot of the reason why people don't achieve goals. And on top of that, though, I think even more important, at least equally as important, is because they don't have the vision on top of their goal. So the goal doesn't have the right meaning. They're not using a goal for what it is. In other words, a goal is not so much about the achievement of it. The goal is just essentially, it's almost like a directional mechanism to make sure you're moving in the right direction. It's not so much about the achievement of the goal. Yeah, of course you'd rather achieve than not achieve, but at the end of the day, the most important thing is that you're moving towards your vision. So I say that for the people who are, you know, the bulk of people who are setting goals and not achieving them, A, you're probably engaged in flawed goal setting, B, and this is a big one, you're probably a bit light on the strategy of the whole thing. In other words, the beauty of the straight line is that it is a technique, it's a technology that allows you to achieve. You know, go out there and set a goal to become a top producer and you don't know how to become a world-class close. Well, good luck being a top. You can wanna be a top producer your whole life. If you don't have the strategy to do it, you're gonna be in trouble. So the straight line to me, in terms of if you're in sales and you're really in business in any way, shape, or form, the straight line is that almost that X factor, it's that really that almost, let's say, the equalize to people who are just getting started and it allows you to achieve your goals more than not achieve your goals. And whether you do or not is really not that important. As long as above those goals, you have a vision that keeps you moving in the right direction. So those two combined, I think are really the core, those three combined, excuse me, they're really the three core reasons that people don't achieve. A, they just engage in flawed goal setting. B, they lack the strategy to get what they want in life. They don't know how to sell and end up becoming closer. They don't have a straight line tactic to close everyone is closable. And three, they don't have the vision on top of that, keep them inspired. And with that comes their why and everything we've already talked about, which essentially keeps you moving in the direction you need to move in when the barriers and obstacles present themselves as they always do. And instead of you getting negative and feeling disempowered, that why and that vision, it allows you to essentially be irresistible and irresistible force moving towards your goal and breaking down the barriers in your way. That's simple. What are some of the things in people's lives that could get in the way potentially of them achieving their vision and their goals? And what advice do you have to deal and manage with those things? Certainly number one, I guess, okay, let's take it to the most basic level. Number one is your physical health. My body's my temple, I shouldn't say it because I kind of polluted the temple pretty bad, but I ended up okay, I got lucky, right? But the point is, is that there's a certain amount of just physical energy that you need in life to go out and do what you want. So if you're out there pawing like a rock star every single night and they're wondering why I can't get what I want the next day, well, maybe you need to take a good look at that. The health is our health is something that we never really think about till we don't have it anymore. But at the heart of all success is the ability to have energy, the energy to go out and work hard and takes hard work. So that's number one. When I was in the seminar business, and I didn't do it for very long, I just didn't feed my spirit to go and give seminars like that. I do trainings, it's very different from what I do. But one of the things that almost held me back from being really great at seminars with people like this average people that are out there is that I'd go out there and I was saying, you gotta work really, really hard. While the average seminar guy says, just take this little box here, it's magical. Give me two grand in the stadium when you're under where plug the box and you'll be rich forever. Now, when I see that, I run the other way. I tell people you gotta work really, really hard and you do. There are no shortcuts to hard work. So then that comes from what? Having the energy, the physical, and body, the spirit to go out there and work really hard. That's number one, people aren't going to do the work. Now, on the energy level, they're physically ill or out of shape and they can't go out and achieve that's number one. Number two, another reason I think people don't achieve their goals or really can't get what they want in life is because they, again, they lack a skill set. They're really not there on the skill level. They don't understand that there's a lot of work that you need to do in drilling and perfecting your honing your craft, so to speak. An actor doesn't go out to Hollywood and try to become a star without taking any acting lessons. They take years and years of classes and lessons and try to improve and hone their skill, right? So if you're not dedicated to becoming great at what you do, then shame on you. In fact, the reason for this program, these tapes, these modules is to actually serve as essentially the food that you need to consume, the knowledge that you need to consume as a salesperson. So you're great at it. I mean, that's it. So I think that's another major reason what that, you have to have that sort of commitment to learning, that commitment to gain knowledge, okay? Without that, it's very, very hard to achieve. I think another major sort of pitfall for people who are out there is that they might have, they have the energy, they have the knowledge, but they have not gotten to the point where they understand the importance of their data. They think they're emotional state. They're emotional cripples. They feel great one day, they feel terrible another day. So they have the strategy, they have the personal power to go do things, but they're like, see-sores. They feel great two days, lousy three days. So essentially they're only the best they can be half the time or less than half the time, right? That holds so many people back from achieving success. So one of the things that we could also go over in these modules is learning how to manage your own state to essentially make sure that you do show up each day and be the best you can possibly when it comes to work and to sales, right? And then lastly, I'd say the one last thing is by your own beliefs that you hold about yourself. If you have, if you're saddled with limiting beliefs, okay? It tends to really, really hold you back from achieving your goals. So you might have everything else in place, but you have some deep-seated belief that maybe I'm not meant for greatness or maybe sales is evil or maybe I'm not good with money or maybe I wasn't meant to be a leader of my company. And those limiting beliefs, if you have those beliefs, you will not, you're almost constitutionally incapable of exceeding the level of your lowest limiting belief. So it's that, you could have everything else is great. Now one belief that holds you back knocks you out of the box and truly succeeding. So again, which is why in these modules, we also do do some work on belief systems and making sure that you have, if you have any limiting beliefs, we root them out to essentially free you up to achieve. And lastly, standards, your personal standards, are you set up for excellence? Is your, do you demand excellence from yourself? You can have everything that's set up perfectly, but your version of success is anything make 80 grand a year. Well, if that's the case, or 40, whatever, 40 grand a year, it doesn't matter what it is. If that's the case, that's all you're gonna make. You end up getting in life what you want, what your standard is. If your standards is very low, good luck trying to achieve greatness in your life. So that's a lot of inner game stuff. Cause again, we have the inner world and outer world, if strategy is intact and our goal setting is being done correctly, if we're not achieving walls, pretty much a good bet that it's inner world stuff that's holding people back. Jordan, thank you so much. I think you've given us a really clear insight into what it means to create a solid vision to give us the direction of where we need to go and how to develop and attain those specific goals to get there. Just a final question. What's your personal vision for the future now? Where do you see yourself in the next few years? You know, it's pretty interesting time for me because of, you know, so much going on right now. I have a movie coming out about my life, right? With Leonardo DiCaprio is playing me. I'm still, I'm not even dead yet. I'm already got Leo playing me, right? So it's pretty interesting. You know, in a sense, I've been struggling. It's a good question over the last, you know, three to six months of really saying to myself, what's next for me? Because I've achieved pretty much everything I've wanted in my life right now at this point. You know, I was coming from such a bad spot when I lost everything. And then I made all my money back and, you know, started empowering lots of people around the world. And I started just feeling so good that it would be easy for me to fall into the trap of saying I just want more of the same. Truth is, I think that I'm better than that for myself. I think I owe myself more. I think I owe the world more. I think I certainly have some great gifts I was given from God or in the age of whatever they are, the ability to inspire people, to empower people. And I think that it would be unfair of me to not sort of share that at a higher level. You know, in fact, one of the reasons why I'm doing this product here, and you know, this is the corporate world, you know, the corporate world is because I couldn't possibly service all the people around the world. And I'm one person, I can't go training, you know, five million people, right? So this product itself allows me to leverage myself to get the message out there, to be able to teach people how to become closers, how to become ethical closers, and how to become visionaries in their own life, and how to inspire themselves, inspire other people, right? But I think that's just, you know, one element of my life. I think the next great element for me is to really, and expand on my charity work that I do. I've done a lot of stuff in Africa right now with impoverished kids, and I actually go in there and use these same lessons and these same strategies and skill sets to, you know, give hope sometimes to what's the hopeless, and hopefully inspire these kids and make a change, and at least some of them have been helped break the cycle of poverty. So, you know, for me, you know, I travel so much around the world, I'm constantly jet lagged, and I always find that matter, how jet lagged I am, when I'm finding myself in a township working with kids, I feel amazing. It feeds my spirit at the highest level. So, I think there's a lot more of that in my future, and I think I really am at that point right now, where probably, you know, we'll have to sort of re-clarify my own vision for the future. I could only imagine what the future holds to me when this movie comes out. I just don't even know what it's gonna be like. So, you know, at this point, I would say, I'm probably got another three to six months on autopilot where I'm at now, because, you know, I'm living my vision, and then I think, you know, I'm always searching around and saying, you know, what's the next conquest? You know, what's the next thing? I don't think I'll, you know, do a Richard Branson and try to blast off into space and try to, you know, put a colony on the moon. I love Richard, he's a friend of mine. I think my future probably lies on the planet still, you know, but maybe in some of the continents, like Africa and, you know, the places around the world where the kids are really, really sorely lacking in this sort of skill sense. I know that I can impart on them that will empower them and really help these communities break the cycles of poverty and really make a change. And that to me would be, you know, huge success and a great sort of finale to my life, because I've kind of pretty much lived 10 or 12 lives that I think I'm going to be able to speak to. And that would be a really great last life, would be to really use the stuff that I once kind of misused and used to get rich myself and turn people into closers, but really wasn't using ethically. And now to take that same stuff and use it at the highest possible ethical level, that to me would be true success in my life. So that's probably the wrong going. Well, we're all excited to see the results of that. It's six months down the line. So Jordan Belfort, thank you very much. Thank you. So everyone, guys, that's our interview here. And there's another thing to actually get out into the field and to be doing it in the real world. It's gonna allow you to become a more effective person, to become a more empowered person, to become a person that's almost a beacon of light, so to speak, in your own family and to everyone around you. Because when you see someone who's a true visionary and you're around them, you know, you almost feel empowered. Like it rubs off on other people. You become a positive force in everyone's life that's around you. So that's the gift you give to yourself and the gift that you give to the people that you love. So take this module and, you know, really, you know, commit it to your heart, commit it to your mind and make it a part of who you are.
